metadata.title: Build a Folder Structure Auditor for Agent Reliability

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Agentic CLI — build it, measure it, take it apart.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Nik Bear Brown. Agentic CLI — build it, measure it, take it apart.

beats.narration_text: The most common agent failure — reading the wrong file — is almost always caused by a folder structure that was designed for humans browsing, not for agents navigating. Report v1, report v2, report final, report final v2 — all in the same folder. The agent picks one. It is wrong half the time. This is not an agent failure. This is a structure failure.

shot.notes: title card: messy folder listing with report_v1.md report_v2.md report_final.md report_final_v2.md all at same level; agent cursor hovering over wrong file; 'structure failure, not agent failure'

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The most common agent failure — reading the wrong file — is almost always caused by a folder structure that was designed for humans browsing, not for agents nav

beats.narration_text: Ask for the auditor. In the terminal: claude — audit this directory structure for agent navigation failure modes. Flag wrong-canonical, over-broad scope, and archive confusion. Suggest minimum restructuring for each flagged issue.

props.command:
claude "Audit this directory structure for agent-navigation
  failure modes. Flag: (1) wrong canonical,
  (2) over-broad scope, (3) archive confusion.
  Suggest minimum restructuring." < directory_tree.txt

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Ask for the auditor. In the terminal: claude — audit this directory structure for agent navigation failure modes. Flag wrong-canonical, over-broad scope, and ar

beats.narration_text: Read the script. It walks the directory tree with pathlib, scores each node for the three failure modes — wrong canonical is triggered when multiple files share a base name with version suffixes; over-broad when more than five similar files live at the same level; archive confusion when dates or version numbers appear beside files without a matching archive folder.

props.code:
# folder_auditor.py — Claude Code output
import pathlib, re, json

VERSION_RE = re.compile(r'(_v\d+|_final|_draft|\d{8})', re.I)

def audit_dir(root):
    results = []
    for p in pathlib.Path(root).rglob('*'):
        risk = []
        siblings = list(p.parent.iterdir())
        base = VERSION_RE.sub('', p.stem)
        matches = [s for s in siblings if base in s.name]
        if len(matches) > 2:
            risk.append('WRONG_CANONICAL')
        if len(siblings) > 10:
            risk.append('OVER_BROAD')
        if risk:
            results.append({'path': str(p), 'risk': risk})
    return results

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Read the script. It walks the directory tree with pathlib, scores each node for the three failure modes — wrong canonical is triggered when multiple files share

beats.narration_text: The audit runs. The directory tree renders, then failure-risk nodes highlight. Config dot py: HIGH risk — no clear canonical version. Secrets dot env: HIGH — should not be at project root. The temp folder: MEDIUM. Pycache: HIGH. Three HIGH risks, one MEDIUM. Restructure before agent use.

Apply the restructuring. In the terminal: claude — update folder_auditor.py to move config dot py into a config slash directory, move secrets dot env to dot env, and re-run the audit to show the risk score dropping.

props.command:
claude "update folder_auditor.py:
  -> move config.py -> config/config.py
  -> move secrets.env -> .env (gitignored)
  -> re-run audit, show risk score drop"

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Apply the restructuring. In the terminal: claude — update folder_auditor.py to move config dot py into a config slash directory, move secrets dot env to dot env

beats.narration_text: Re-run. Config dot py: flag gone. Secrets dot env: flag gone. The remaining flags are pycache — always exclude from agent scope — and temp, which gets a cleanup note. Two of four risks resolved in one restructuring pass.

shot.notes: before/after audit comparison: left column shows 3 HIGH + 1 MED flags, right column shows 1 HIGH + 1 MED after restructure; green check marks on resolved items

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Re-run. Config dot py: flag gone. Secrets dot env: flag gone. The remaining flags are pycache — always exclude from agent scope — and temp, which gets a cleanup

beats.narration_text: Folder structure is agent communication. The layout tells the agent which files are current, which are archived, and what is in scope. A structure optimized for human browsing can be catastrophic for agent navigation — because the agent has no context about which report_final is actually final.

shot.notes: teardown card: 'folder structure = agent communication'; two columns — 'human browse' vs 'agent navigate'; human: any file works, agent: canonical = the only file

beats.narration_text: Your move: run this auditor on a real project directory. Start with the folders your agent touches most. Next reel: we build the session state generator — because the structure of time matters as much as the structure of space.

shot.notes: next steps: '1) run auditor on your project root  2) prioritize folders agent reads most  3) archive before deleting'; teaser: 'next: session state generator'
